description_long: This is a medieval-style black metal helmet with a full-face visor and a pronounced central ridge running from the top to the bottom of the faceplate. The helmet features a flared neck guard with riveted edges and a slightly angular, faceted design that gives it a robust and intimidating appearance. The visor has a narrow horizontal slit for vision and is adorned with small decorative perforations near the mouth area. The surface of the helmet is matte black with subtle metallic highlights, and the construction appears to be solid and protective. Brass or bronze rivets are visible along the lower edge, securing the neck guard to the main body. There are no visible logos, graphics, or modern embellishments, emphasizing its utilitarian and historical character.
description: A robust, matte black medieval helmet with a full-face visor, central ridge, and riveted flared neck guard. The design is angular and solid, with minimal decoration.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full-face visor with a narrow eye slit, central ridge, and decorative perforations near the mouth.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the helmet's angular profile, flared neck guard, and the attachment rivets along the lower edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the pronounced central ridge running down the back and the full circumference of the riveted neck guard.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, showing the visor's side profile, rivets, and the helmet's overall faceted shape.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Small decorative perforations near the mouth area on the visor; no other visible vents.
